title: D2 encryption domains spec
description: Schema for contracts/encryption-domains/encryption-domains.spec.json — the source-of-truth catalog of encryption-domain identifiers. Each domain corresponds to an independently-rotated keyring registered via services.AddD2EncryptionFor(domain, factory). The 'plaintext' sentinel is included as a closed-catalog entry so spec validation on mq-messages.spec's 'encryption' field cleanly enumerates the only acceptable values. DcsvIo.D2.EncryptionDomains.SourceGen reads it to emit EncryptionDomains into DcsvIo.D2.Encryption; the TypeScript codegen pipeline reads it to emit encryption-domains.g.ts into @dcsv-io/d2-encryption-abstractions.

allOf.$comment: A sealed domain MUST name its single decrypting consumer service.
consumerService is meaningful ONLY for sealed domains; its presence without mode:sealed is a spec error.
The plaintext sentinel is never encrypted, so it carries no mode.

constName.description: Public C# / TS constant identifier — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Must be unique across the spec.

value.description: Wire-format domain identifier value. Must be unique across the spec.

mode.description: Encryption mode for the domain. 'symmetric' (default when absent — strict back-compat) = shared-keyring AES-256-GCM (version-1 frame), every grant-holder both encrypts and decrypts. 'sealed' = per-consumer-service ephemeral-static ECDH (version-2 frame), producers seal to the recipient service's public key and only that service opens. Forbidden on the 'plaintext' sentinel.

consumerService.description: The single decrypting recipient's ServiceId (SpiffeWorkloadIdentity.ServiceId), lowercase [a-z0-9-]. REQUIRED iff mode == 'sealed'; FORBIDDEN otherwise. The routing anchor: the messaging composer resolves domain → consumerService → that service's public seal key, and the seal-open authority check reads it to know whose private key may be fetched. Two sealed domains consumed by the same service share one keypair (per-service reuse).

doc.description: XML <summary> / JSDoc text rendered on the emitted constant.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
